title: Golf club head
abstract: A golf club head has a body (5) having a club face (10) for contacting a ball, and a rear portion (11) substantially opposite the club face (10). A force-transfer member (34) within the body has a first end (36) at or adjacent the club face, and a second end (37) in communication with the rear portion (11) of the body. The force-transfer member (34) is arranged to transfer force from the club face (10) to the rear portion (11) of the body when the club face (10) strikes a ball.
claims: Claims 1. A golf club head comprising : a body having a club face for contacting a ball, and a rear portion substantially opposite the club face; and a force-transfer member within the body, the force-transfer member having a first end at or adjacent the club face, and a second end in communication with the rear portion of the body, the force-transfer member arranged to transfer force from the club face to the rear portion of the body when the club face strikes a ball. 2. The golf club head according to claim 1, wherein the force-transfer member comprises an elongate portion extending between the first end of the force- transfer member and the second end of the force-transfer member. 3. The golf club head according to claim 2, wherein a portion of the body surrounding the elongate portion of the force-transfer member is substantially hollow. 4. The golf club head according to claim 2 or claim 3, wherein an axis of the golf club head passes from an optimal point of impact of the club face and through a centre of gravity of the golf club head, and the elongate portion of the force-transfer member is substantially coaxial with the axis. 5. The golf club head according to claim 4, wherein the axis of the golf club head passes through the rear portion of the golf club head. 6. The golf club head according to claim 4 or claim 5, wherein the optimal point of impact is located substantially at a centre of the club face. 7. The golf club head according to any one claims 2 to 6, wherein the elongate portion of the force-transfer member extends at an angle of about 90Â° from the club face in a vertical plane. 8. The golf club head according to any one of claims 2 to 6, wherein the elongate portion of the force-transfer member extends at an angle of less than 90Â° from the club face in a vertical plane. 9. The golf club head according to any one of claims 2 to 8, wherein the elongate portion of the force-transfer member extends at an angle of about 90Â° from the club face in a horizontal plane. 10. The golf club head according to any one of claims 2 to 8, wherein the elongate portion of the force-transfer member extends at an angle of less than 90Â° from the club face in a horizontal plane. 11. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the first end of the force-transfer member comprises a tapered portion, the tapered portion tapering outwardly toward the club face. 12. The golf club head according to claim 11, wherein an outer part of the taper is substantially the same size and shape as the club face. 13. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the second end of the force-transfer member comprises a plate arranged to transfer force to the rear portion of the body. 14. The golf club head according to claim 13, wherein the plate is shaped to substantially match a curvature of an inner surface of the rear portion of the body. 15. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the force- transfer member passes through a centre of gravity of the golf club head. 16. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 15, further comprising a shock-absorbent member located between the second end of the force-transfer member and the rear portion of the body. 17. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the force- transfer member is integrally formed with the club face. 18. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the force- transfer member further comprises a weighted portion. 19. The golf club head according to claim 18, wherein the weighted portion is arranged to lower a centre of gravity of the golf club head. 20. The golf club head according to claim 18 or claim 19, wherein the weighted portion is integrally formed with the tapered portion of the force-transfer member. 21. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the force- transfer member is formed from titanium. 22. The golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 21, comprising a removable adjustment component. 23. The golf club head according to claim 22, wherein the removable adjustment component is arranged to transfer force from the second end of the force transfer member to the rear portion of the body when the club face strikes a ball. 24. The golf club head according to claim 22 or 23, wherein the removable adjustment component comprises a shock-absorbent member having a first property. 25. The golf club head according to claim 24, wherein the removable adjustment component is removable from the golf club head and is replaceable with an alternative removable adjustment component that comprises a shock-absorbent member having a second property that differs from the first property. 26. The golf club head according to claim 25, wherein the first property and second property comprise stiffness, to enable adjustment of the Coefficient of Restitution of the golf club head. 27. The golf club head according to any one of claims 22 to 26, wherein the removable adjustment component comprises a sleeve, and wherein the second end of the force transfer member is telescopically received in the sleeve when the removable adjustment component is engaged with the golf club head. 28. A golf club comprising a shaft connected to the golf club head according to any one of claims 1 to 27. 29. The golf club according to claim 28, wherein the angle of the club face relative to a longitudinal axis of the shaft defines a loft angle of the club face. 30. The golf club according to claim 29, wherein an angle at which the force-transfer member extends from the club face is proportional to the loft angle. 31. The golf club according to any one of claims 28 to 30 when dependent on claim 2, wherein the elongate portion of the force-transfer member is arranged to substantially align with a direction of movement of the golf club head when the golf club is swung by a user. 32. The combination of a golf club according to any one of claims 28 to 31, and a plurality of removable adjustment components to adjust the performance of the golf club head.
